entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: Grain sorghum, Sorghum bicolor (L.) Moench, grown in semi-arid tropics

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain produced per unit area, used as a measure of performance

entities.name: YIELD STABILITY
entities.description: The consistency of grain yield across different environments

entities.name: ADAPTABILITY
entities.description: The ability of sorghum population types to adjust to unpredictable drought and stress factors

entities.name: DROUGHT
entities.description: A stress factor characterized by lack of water in semi-arid areas

entities.name: SEMI-ARID TROPICS
entities.description: Environmental region characterized by unpredictable rainfall and drought

entities.name: MAKUENI DISTRICT
entities.description: A specific district in Kenya where the research was conducted

entities.name: KENYA
entities.description: The country where the research was conducted

entities.name: HETEROZYGOSITY
entities.description: The state of having different alleles at a locus, investigated for its effect on adaptability

entities.name: HETEROGENEITY
entities.description: The state of being diverse in character or content, investigated for its effect on adaptability

entities.name: PARENT LINES
entities.description: Homozygous lines used as genetic material for creating hybrids and blends

entities.name: SINGLE-CROSS HYBRIDS
entities.description: Offspring of a cross between two homozygous parent lines

entities.name: TWO-COMPONENT BLENDS
entities.description: Mixtures consisting of two parent lines

entities.name: TWO-COMPONENT HYBRID BLENDS
entities.description: Mixtures consisting of hybrids and parent lines

entities.name: COMBINED ANALYSES OF VARIANCE
entities.description: Statistical method used to analyze data across multiple environments

entities.name: REGRESSION ANALYSIS
entities.description: Statistical method used to derive stability parameters

entities.name: PATTERN ANALYSIS
entities.description: Analytical approach using classification and ordination techniques to analyze entry means

entities.name: CLASSIFICATION TECHNIQUES
entities.description: Methods used within pattern analysis to distinguish homozygous from heterozygous entries

entities.name: ORDINATION TECHNIQUES
entities.description: Methods used within pattern analysis to visualize data patterns via biplots

entities.name: PRINCIPAL COMPONENTS ANALYSIS
entities.description: Statistical method used to identify main variance drivers, with components correlated to yield and stability

entities.name: ENTRY X ENVIRONMENT INTERACTION
entities.description: The variation in performance of different genotypes across different environments

entities.name: HETEROZYGOUS ENTRIES
entities.description: Plant materials with heterozygous genotypes, showing superiority in yield and stability

entities.name: HOMOZYGOUS ENTRIES
entities.description: Plant materials with homozygous genotypes

entities.name: HETEROGENEOUS ENTRIES
entities.description: Plant materials with mixed genotypes
